data.description: Site currently has no favicon (index.html has no <link rel='icon'>). Hand-author an SVG of the octahedron geometry silhouette in Fractal brand palette, ship it as /public/favicon.svg + fallback /public/favicon.ico (or PNG), and wire the <link rel='icon'> + apple-touch-icon tags into index.html.\n\nWhy SVG: crisp at all sizes, tiny file, no build step, matches the brand's geometric language.
data.title: Add favicon: hand-authored SVG octahedron silhouette

data.body: Shipped: hand-authored favicon.svg (4-color octahedron diamond) + rasters (16/32 PNG, 180 apple-touch, multi-res ICO) generated via scripts/build-favicon.mjs (sharp + png-to-ico installed cleanly). index.html updated with 4 link tags. typecheck/build pass; /favicon.svg, /favicon.ico, /apple-touch-icon.png all serve with correct MIME on dev server. 7 pre-existing test failures (Neighborhood layout) unrelated and present on master.

PASS. Reviewed cold against plan. Findings: (1) SVG matches the plan's reference code exactly — 4 polygons + 1 outline path in a 32-unit viewBox, transparent bg. (2) All 4 hex colors verified against src/data/houses.ts: events.deep #C13B2A, school.light #C41E20, campus.light #2E6B4A, lab.light #E870A0 — zero faux hexes. (3) pnpm typecheck clean, pnpm build clean. dist/ contains favicon.svg, favicon.ico, favicon-16.png, favicon-32.png, apple-touch-icon.png. (4) Tests: 7 pre-existing Neighborhood failures, no new ones introduced. (5) index.html link order is correct: SVG -> PNG 32 -> PNG 16 -> apple-touch-icon, inserted after <title> and before Google Fonts preconnect, per plan. (6) build:favicon script in package.json is standalone — NOT wired into pnpm build, per plan. devDeps sharp + png-to-ico added correctly. (7) Visually inspected rasters via Read: 180px apple-touch-icon is sharp with all 4 distinct colors and a bold charcoal outline; 32px is legible as a quartered diamond; 16px holds up — silhouette and color zones still read. No need for the single-color fallback the plan offered. (8) No stray files (.DS_Store, etc.). PRD doesn't mention favicons (universally expected, no contradiction). All acceptance criteria met short of live-browser tab strip / iOS Add-to-Home-Screen, which require runtime. Moving to done.
